title: Oil Prices Go Down as US and Iran Might Talk Peace
summary: Oil prices went down a little on Tuesday because people hope the US and Iran will have new peace talks. This hope makes people less worried about problems with oil supplies. On Monday, oil prices went up after the US blocked Iran's ports when talks failed. But then, President Trump said Iran called to make a deal. A news report said Iran offered to stop some nuclear work for five years, but the US wanted 20 years. Experts think this could mean less fighting and lower oil prices. However, oil is still more expensive than before the war started in February. The International Energy Agency says April might be worse because no new oil is being loaded.

How long did Iran offer to stop nuclear work?
What does the International Energy Agency say about April?
Is oil cheaper or more expensive than before the war started?

background_read: Oil is a natural resource used for energy, like in cars and factories. When countries fight or have problems, it can affect how much oil is available, which changes the price. The US and Iran have been in a conflict, and peace talks are meetings to try to stop the fighting and make things better. This article talks about how hope for these talks made oil prices go down a little.
Article_Structure: WHO: The US, Iran, President Trump, experts, and the International Energy Agency
WHAT: Oil prices went down because of hope for new peace talks between the US and Iran
WHERE: In global markets and between countries like the US and Iran
WHY: To explain how peace talks can affect oil prices and reduce worries about supplies

perspectives.description: Some people think new talks could lead to less fighting and lower oil prices.
